summary: Frontend-only Phase 1 premium homepage test. 19/20 assertions PASS. Header, flame logo + 'Cape Ember COFFEE CO.' text, sticky-shrink-on-scroll, 4 product cards with prices/roast tags, testimonials w/ 15 stars, newsletter form, trust badges, footer (logo + WhatsApp + 18 links), mobile menu, and nav routes (/shop /about /subscriptions /brew-guide) all work. Add-to-Cart correctly opens AuthModal for unauthenticated users. ONE CRITICAL UI BUG: the Hero 'OUR STORY' button (btn-secondary) renders with dark-brown text on the dark hero photograph making it nearly invisible — Tailwind text-white is overridden by the global .btn-secondary{color:var(--text-primary)} rule in index.css.

ui_bugs.issue: Hero 'OUR STORY' button is unreadable. Inline classes are 'btn-secondary border-white/50 text-white ...' but computed color resolves to rgb(44,26,18) (dark brown) instead of white. Root cause: .btn-secondary in /app/frontend/src/index.css line 200 sets color:var(--text-primary) with the same specificity as Tailwind utilities — and since the global stylesheet is processed after Tailwind in the cascade for these particular utilities here, text-white loses. On the dark hero image the text is invisible (only the bordered rectangle is visible). Fix options: (a) override inline with arbitrary !important — className="btn-secondary border-white/50 !text-white hover:bg-white hover:!text-[#2C1A12]"; or (b) add a new variant class like .btn-secondary-light in index.css with color:#fff; or (c) replace btn-secondary with btn-primary-outline on the hero. Same risk applies anywhere btn-secondary is paired with text-white over a dark background.

issues: Secondary 'Our Story' button has insufficient contrast against dark hero image (dark text on dark photo) — see ui_bug above. From the design-system point of view, btn-secondary's default dark text only works on the light cream background; a hero/dark-bg variant is needed.

action_items: [HIGH] Fix hero 'Our Story' button text colour (see ui_bug). Use !text-white or create btn-secondary-light variant. Apply same fix anywhere btn-secondary appears on a dark background.

[LOW] Newsletter form has no submit handler wired to a backend endpoint — clicking Subscribe currently submits the form via the browser default and reloads the page. If a real subscribe API exists, wire it up; otherwise prevent default + show a toast.
[LOW] Hero scroll indicator 'SCROLL' is purely decorative; consider making it a button/anchor that smooth-scrolls to the products section for accessibility.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/index.css .btn-secondary color uses var(--text-primary) without a light variant; this is the source of the hero CTA contrast bug.
HomePage.js newsletter <form> has no onSubmit handler — the form will reload the page if submitted; either wire it to /api/newsletter or add e.preventDefault().
HomePage.js uses two AuthModal instances (one in Navbar, one inline) — duplicate state could be unified, but functionally correct.
HomePage.js line 12-14: hero/roasting/karoo Unsplash URLs are hard-coded; move to a constants file or CMS so non-devs can change them.
Footer 'whatsapp' button text check passed via testid; cosmetic — there is no visible text label next to the WA icon in the floating CTA which may hurt accessibility (aria-label is present though).
test_credentials: testuser2@capeember.co.za / Test1234! (not used — add-to-cart correctly triggers auth modal so login flow was not required for Phase 1 homepage testing)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1 premium homepage is essentially complete. ONLY blocker is the hero 'Our Story' button colour. After fix, re-verify with: page.evaluate("window.getComputedStyle(document.querySelector('[data-testid=hero-story-btn]')).color") — must return rgb(255,255,255) before hover. Everything else (logo flame asset, header shrink on scroll, 4 product cards, testimonials, newsletter form presence, trust badges, footer, mobile menu, nav links to /shop /about /subscriptions /brew-guide) is confirmed working at 1920x1080 and 390x844.
rca of the issue: Hero secondary CTA: index.css line 194-209 defines .btn-secondary with color:var(--text-primary). When HomePage.js applies className='btn-secondary ... text-white', both rules have selector specificity (0,1,0). CSS resolves the conflict by source order — and because Tailwind's PostCSS pipeline places the global index.css custom component layer AFTER the utilities layer in the final stylesheet, .btn-secondary wins. Result: text colour remains var(--text-primary)=#2C1A12 (dark brown) overlaid on a dark hero photo, rendering the label invisible. Reproduction: open /, observe two CTAs under 'Roasted with Passion'. Right CTA shows only an outlined rectangle. Mitigation: use Tailwind's important prefix `!text-white` (compiled to color:#fff !important) on the className, OR add a `.btn-secondary-on-dark` class in index.css with `color:#fff`, OR move the color declaration in btn-secondary into a higher-specificity selector and let utilities override.
